mechanism_narrative: Industrial production is a direct measure of real economic output; sustained increases indicate expanding activity and rising corporate earnings, driving equity prices higher over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases are associated with negative or flat SPX forward returns in more than 40% of monthly observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment falling below 3.5% signals an extremely tight labor market that fuels wage inflation and squeezes corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns in the following week as markets price in higher policy risk.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE < 3.5% the average 5-day SPX return is positive or zero, or if the win rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus annualized CPI inflation) exceeds 2%, monetary policy is considered restrictive and suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, resulting in negative forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: If the real rate threshold triggers produce positive SPX returns more than 40% of the time over any 3-year period, or if the average return difference between trigger and non-trigger periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: During a labor market recovery phase, a declining unemployment rate confirms improving economic conditions and rising consumer confidence, supporting a positive equity outlook over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines are followed by negative SPX 60-day returns more than 35% of the time in expansionary regimes defined by NBER dates.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million annualized units reflect strong real estate demand and broader economic momentum, supporting positive corporate earnings and stock prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 1500 (annualized) leads to negative SPX returns in more than 40% of monthly windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises above 103 (index level representing above-trend output), the manufacturing sector is in a boom phase, which boosts industrial earnings and stock prices in the very short term.
failure_condition: If INDPRO > 103 produces average 5-day SPX returns not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment indicate synchronized consumer and labor market strength, which should lift corporate revenues and equity prices over a month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS > prior month and PAYEMS > prior month
failure_condition: If the triggered condition results in negative or flat SPX returns more than 50% of instances over a 5-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more over three months is a classic recession indicator; equity markets react negatively to rising joblessness as it signals fundamental economic weakness over the coming quarter.
failure_condition: If rising UNRATE triggers are followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 30% of cases over any full business cycle.
